scenario: Vertex Brokerage runs Camunda 8.8 with one Spring Boot worker fleet processing 12000 transactions per day. The legacy code still sets camunda.client.identity.audience for OIDC token requests; the team must point it at the replacement property documented in the Spring Boot starter reference now that identity is folded into Camunda.
question: Which property replaces the legacy camunda.client.identity.audience in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Set camunda.client.zeebe.audience to carry the OIDC audience claim under the zeebe configuration subtree
Set camunda.client.auth.audience to carry the OIDC audience claim under the auth configuration subtree
Set camunda.client.cloud.audience to carry the OIDC audience claim under the cloud configuration subtree
Set camunda.client.operate.audience to carry the OIDC audience claim under the operate configuration subtree

a.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.zeebe.audience is itself listed as deprecated and its replacement is also camunda.client.auth.audience; it is not the documented replacement for the identity audience alias.

b.text: Correct. The reference table lists camunda.client.auth.audience as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.identity.audience property in Camunda 8.8, alongside camunda.client.auth.scope for the identity scope alias.

c.text: Incorrect. The cloud subtree carries SaaS cluster fields such as cluster-id and region; the audience replacement lives under camunda.client.auth, not under cloud.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no camunda.client.operate.audience property documented in the reference; the audience replacement lives under camunda.client.auth.

explanation: Identity properties in the starter reference are grouped as deprecated because identity is now part of Camunda. The audience claim moves to camunda.client.auth.audience and the scope alias moves to camunda.client.auth.scope in Camunda 8.8.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
